question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the grpcServerConfigurer does not configure any maxHeaderListSize limit on the NettyServerBuilder to send gRPC requests with thousands of custom metadata headers, causing HPACK decompression to consume excessive CPU and memory, effectively partitioning the server from legitimate clients by saturating the event loop?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any connection-level backpressure enforcement in the grpcServerConfigurer to open many connections simultaneously during a partition recovery window, causing the Netty accept queue to overflow and silently dropping connection attempts from legitimate clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of permitKeepAliveTime configuration on the NettyServerBuilder to send keepalive pings at an extremely high frequency, causing the server to respond to each ping and saturating the Netty event loop with PING frame processing, creating a network partition effect for all other connected clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any connection count limit in the grpcServerConfigurer to open connections faster than the server can process GOAWAY frames during a partition recovery, causing the server's file descriptor table to be exhausted and preventing any new connections including health check probes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the applicationTaskExecutor is shared across all gRPC connections to cause priority inversion by opening many connections with 5 streams each that perform slow database-backed operations, starving the executor of threads needed to process keepalive or health check requests and causing the server to appear partitioned to monitoring systems?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any TLS mutual authentication requirement in the grpcServerConfigurer to impersonate a legitimate client during a partition recovery window, establishing connections that consume all available call slots before real clients can reconnect?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of maxConnectionAge on the NettyServerBuilder to maintain a connection established before a network partition event indefinitely after the partition heals, preventing the server from enforcing any connection refresh policy and potentially serving stale or inconsistent state to that client?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any HTTP/2 WINDOW_UPDATE rate limiting in the grpcServerConfigurer to rapidly alternate between sending WINDOW_UPDATE frames that open and close the flow control window, causing the server's Netty pipeline to thrash between blocked and unblocked states and degrading throughput for all other clients on the server?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any server-side call deadline in the grpcServerConfigurer to submit a gRPC call that triggers a long-running database transaction via the transactionOperationsReadOnly bean, holding a database connection open indefinitely and exhausting the DB connection pool, which then partitions the gRPC service from its database backend?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the read-only TransactionTemplate configured without any timeout to trigger a long-running read transaction that holds a database connection open during a network partition between the gRPC service and the database, causing the connection pool to be exhausted and all subsequent gRPC calls requiring DB access to fail?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a transaction timeout on the TransactionTemplate created in transactionOperationsReadOnly() to cause a read transaction to remain open indefinitely during a simulated DB network partition, blocking other transactions from acquiring locks and creating a cascading failure that partitions the entire gRPC service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the read-only transaction template (which does not set a timeout via transactionTemplate.setTimeout()) to hold open a database read transaction across a network partition event, preventing the database from reclaiming the connection and causing a connection pool starvation that denies service to all other gRPC clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any server-side flow control window size configuration in the grpcServerConfigurer to send a very large HTTP/2 DATA frame that exceeds the default flow control window, causing the Netty server to buffer the excess data in memory and potentially triggering an OutOfMemoryError that partitions the server from all clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any connection-level timeout in the grpcServerConfigurer to establish a TCP connection to port 5600 and then send no data (silent connection), causing the server to hold the connection in a pending state indefinitely and consuming a file descriptor and Netty channel object for each such silent connection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any HTTP/2 preface timeout in the grpcServerConfigurer to open a TCP connection and send only a partial HTTP/2 client preface, causing the server to wait indefinitely for the rest of the preface and holding the connection open, allowing an attacker to exhaust file descriptors with many such partial-preface connections?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the grpcServerConfigurer sets only maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection without setting any total server-wide concurrent call limit, allowing an attacker to open N connections each with 5 streams to consume N*5 executor threads, where N is only bounded by OS file descriptor limits, causing complete executor exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any HEADERS frame rate limiting in the grpcServerConfigurer to rapidly open and close gRPC streams (sending HEADERS then immediate RST_STREAM), causing the server to allocate and deallocate stream state at a rate that saturates the Netty event loop and creates a network partition effect for legitimate streaming clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any server-side write timeout in the grpcServerConfigurer to subscribe to a high-volume topic stream and then stop reading responses, causing the server's Netty write buffer to fill, blocking the applicationTaskExecutor thread assigned to that stream, and eventually causing a cascading thread starvation that partitions the server from all other clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any connection-level memory limit in the grpcServerConfigurer to open many connections each with 5 streams that request large message payloads, causing the server to allocate large buffers per stream and triggering a JVM GC storm that pauses all threads and creates a network partition effect for all connected clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any server-side PING enforcement (no keepAliveTime set) to prevent the server from detecting that a client has become unreachable due to a network partition, causing the server to continue buffering messages for the dead client and exhausting heap memory that would otherwise serve live clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frame limit in the grpcServerConfigurer to send a HEADERS frame followed by an unbounded sequence of CONTINUATION frames (CVE-2023-44487 style), causing the server to buffer all continuation data before processing, exhausting heap memory and partitioning the server from all clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any server-side stream priority or weight configuration in the grpcServerConfigurer to send HTTP/2 PRIORITY frames that create a dependency tree causing the server's stream scheduler to spend excessive CPU time on priority calculations, degrading throughput for all other streams and creating a network partition effect?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any connection-level error rate limiting in the grpcServerConfigurer to repeatedly send malformed gRPC frames that trigger connection-level errors and GOAWAY responses, causing the server to spend excessive resources on error handling and connection teardown, effectively partitioning the server from legitimate clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any server-side stream count enforcement beyond maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection in the grpcServerConfigurer to send HTTP/2 HEADERS frames with stream IDs that exceed the server's expected range, causing the server to allocate stream state for invalid stream IDs and exhausting memory?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any server-side connection age limit in the grpcServerConfigurer to maintain a connection established during normal operation through a network partition event and back, receiving messages that were buffered during the partition without the server being able to enforce any freshness or consistency guarantees on the resumed stream?
